steps:
Monday: boxes = 45, chocolates per box = n → total chocolates = 45n.
Tuesday: boxes = 3 × 45 = 135, chocolates per box = n − 4 → total chocolates = 135(n − 4).
Combined total: 45n + 135(n − 4) = 4860.
Simplify: 45n + 135n − 540 = 4860 → 180n − 540 = 4860 → 180n = 5400 → n = 30.
Check options: n = 30 is option D.
But verify: if n = 30, Monday chocolates = 45×30 = 1350, Tuesday chocolates = 135×(26) = 3510, total = 4860 ✓.
However, check if any other option also works? Plug each option into equation 180n − 540 = 4860:
A) n=24 → 180×24 − 540 = 4320 − 540 = 3780 ≠ 4860.
B) n=26 → 180×26 − 540 = 4680 − 540 = 4140 ≠ 4860.
C) n=28 → 180×28 − 540 = 5040 − 540 = 4500 ≠ 4860.
D) n=30 → 180×30 − 540 = 5400 − 540 = 4860 ✓.
E) n=32 → 180×32 − 540 = 5760 − 540 = 5220 ≠ 4860.
Only D gives 4860. Wait, earlier I wrote answer C but calculation shows D. Correct answer is D.
